calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE

Appearances via Zoom:
Roy Enav of Quill & Arrow, LLP (310.933.4271, renav@quillarrowlaw.com) for plaintiff and moving party;
Mikaela Critchlow of Nelson Mullins Riley & Scarborough LLP (619.489.6174, mikaela.critchlow@nelsonmullins.com) for defendant and responding party.

The court did not receive notice the matter was contested but hears the matter as both sides are present. Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: The motion to compel further responses to request for production is granted in part. Defendant Polestar Automotive USA Inc. shall provide a further response to request for production number 30 by producing nonprivileged documents found pursuant to the search terms set out in the March 26, 2026 email from Briseida Henriquez to Mike Hurvitz et al., attached as Exhibit 1 to the Roy Enav Declaration. Polestar shall make production by June 10, 2026. No sanctions are awarded. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Mar-24-2026 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: motion to compel further responses to requests for production, numbers 30 and 31, is denied in part and continued in part for further hearing to April 28, 2026.

With respect to number 30, Cordoba is entitled to receive documents reflecting other customers' complaints similar to the alleged defects claimed by plaintiff, limited to vehicles purchased in California for the same year, make and model of the subject vehicle. These documents are relevant to Polestar's knowledge of the defects. However, Cordoba's request is too vague for Polestar to ascertain the similarity of other complaints. The request does not define the alleged defects claimed by plaintiff. The parties are ordered to meet and confer within one week. Cordoba must commence by defining a reasonable scope of "similar complaints." If Cordoba fails to do so, Polestar has no further production obligation.

With respect to number 31, Polestar asserted objections but made a full representation of compliance: "Subject to and without waiving the foregoing objections, Polestar will comply with this request in full and produce the NHTSA recall documents applicable to the SUBJECT VEHICLE (bates-stamped CORDOBA000001 - CORDOBA000061)." Cordoba does not inform the court what, if anything, is wrong with this representation of compliance. Relief is denied as to this RFP.

The court "shall" sanction the party who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents unless the court finds that the unsuccessful party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h).) No party has requested sanctions. The court gives notice to both parties that it may impose reasonable sanctions upon either party at the continued hearing date if it finds a misuse of the discovery process. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
